_comment: Tier-3 community setup guides derived from real GameNative listings on EmuReady (each carries a real sourceUrl backlink). Tier 3 = 'From the community', NOT tier 1 (tier 1 = the user's own device-verified data). No GameNative <Game>_config.json blob is attached: EmuReady listings do not carry the full flat config, and the only public 'snapshot' repo (andreisugu/gamenative-config-tools) ships PLACEHOLDER example data, so fabricating one would be dishonest. dataAsOf: 2026-06-08.

steps.text: Install GameNative.
Community report: Great on Dimensity 7300.
Install GameNative.
Community note (via EmuReady): Performance is between 54-60fps even with high performance mode and fan at full speed.
Community report: Great on Dimensity 8300.
Install GameNative.

Community note (via EmuReady): Got it to work with gamenative 1.0

Make sure to set executable path to KINGDOM HEARTS HD 1.5+2.5 ReMIX.exe not KINGDOM HEARTS 1.5+2.5 Launcher.exe

Set dx wrapper to vkd3d and vk3d3 version 3.0b
Community report: Great on Snapdragon 8 Elite.
Install GameNative.

Community note (via EmuReady): Takes a sec to load and some tinkering, but is playable. Under graphics I set "Present Modes" to fifo. In game I set it to fullscreen (try disabling fullscreen all together if the game crashes or anything) and disabled particle effects, and if you're having issues with the controller enable Steam Input. Takes maybe 30 seconds to load but does play fine enough once loaded

Community note (via EmuReady): Average temps sit around 75 to 78s with the fan set to smart mode.

Game is able to reach 60fps but that's pretty much only during the beginning, the moment 2-3 enemies appear on screen it starts to struggle and falls to 45-50fps.
The more enemies the worse it gets, on the village section for example it falls to 30fps and becomes unplayable due to slowdown.

When locking the fps to 30 via the in-g
Community report: Playable on Snapdragon 8 Gen 2.

Community note (via EmuReady): Using Clustertune Small underclock setting. 40 fps to 60 fps in a few areas with age age temp at 80°C
Tiny bit of frame stuttering but in no way game breaking and fully playable!
All in game settings low and AMD 2.0 upscaling set to performance
Framegen optional for more stable fps

Community note (via EmuReady): I get 40/50 FPS with stutters down to 20. Better to lock it at stable 30 FPS. 
Plus, using Lossless Scaling, you can easily reach a stable 60 FPS.

Community note (via EmuReady): Using current drivers (as of 5/20/2026) solid FPS with some dips here and there. Most notable graphical glitching (Foliage/Shadows Loading with black graphical jittering), Resolution Scaling Set to 75% with FSR Sharpness. Running a Small Underclock using ClusterTune. Using Native Android screen record to record (Takes anywhere from 2%-5% away from Native Performance)

Community note (via EmuReady): To use SenPatcher and its fixes:
Open container and install it first with the .exe (or manually) - the .exe will handily pick up the right place
Then make sure you set this variable in the "Environment" tab of the container settings
WINEDLLOVERRIDES
DINPUT8=n,b

NOTE: once this variable is set, the executable path must be ed8.exe - SenLauncher will no longer launch with it set. To change settings,

Community note (via EmuReady): When starting the game make sure to go into settings to turn on the controller otherwise your not moving anywhere.

Community note (via EmuReady): First you will need to go into the game then Options then Video then Widescreen 16:9. Then Advanced, set Shader, Model/Texture Detail and Effect Detail to Medium. Helps reduce stuttering and makes it run 120FPS no problem. Perfectly playable and looks great. This also works for Portal Reloaded.

Community note (via EmuReady): This game runs like dog-water, but that isn't any fault of the tablet or the emulator. This game is doing its best to be faithful to the PS1 release, but that means it has a bunch of limitations that are hard to deal with in modern times, such as a 15 FPS cap while in combat.

Community note (via EmuReady): Copied from rain4cat's report:
"**IMPORTANT:**
On the first boot, you must change a setting, otherwise you literally can't move UP in the menu and in-game you will constantly move up.
To fix this, open Undertale and do the following while on the main menu:
Settings > Joystick Config > Change DIR CHOICE to ANALOG ONLY"
Thanks to them for helping get the game to work!

Community note (via EmuReady): Plays, and even plays around 30fps, but even with frameskip on, the engine can't keep the framerate and frame time in lock step, so you get slow downs where a 6 second cooldown can take as much as 12 seconds. The game remains responsive and playable during this slowdown, just events take longer. The further you get in the game the worse it is. The first map is almost completely playable.
